items.name: Argentina
facts: Argentina is home to wide grasslands called the Pampas.
Part of the Andes Mountains runs along Argentina.

items.name: Australia
facts: Australia is both a country and part of the smallest continent.
It is known for animals like kangaroos and koalas.

items.name: Brazil
facts: Brazil is the largest country in South America.
Much of the Amazon Rainforest is in Brazil.

items.name: Canada
facts: Canada is the second-largest country in the world by area.
It has many lakes and long snowy winters.

items.name: China
facts: China has one of the largest populations in the world.
The Great Wall is one of its famous landmarks.

items.name: Egypt
facts: Egypt is known for the pyramids and the Nile River.
Most of Egypt's people live near the Nile.

items.name: France
facts: France is famous for the Eiffel Tower.
Paris is one of the most visited cities in the world.

items.name: Germany
facts: Germany is in central Europe.
It is known for forests, castles, and engineering.

items.name: India
facts: India is one of the most populated countries in the world.
The Taj Mahal is one of India's famous landmarks.

items.name: Italy
facts: Italy is shaped a bit like a boot on the map.
Rome has many ancient buildings and ruins.

items.name: Japan
facts: Japan is made up of islands in East Asia.
It is known for cherry blossoms and fast trains.

items.name: Kenya
facts: Kenya is known for savannas and wildlife.
Mount Kenya is one of Africa's highest mountains.

items.name: Mexico
facts: Mexico is south of the United States.
It has beaches, deserts, forests, and mountains.

items.name: New Zealand
facts: New Zealand is made up of two main islands.
It is known for green landscapes and sheep farms.

items.name: Nigeria
facts: Nigeria has the largest population in Africa.
Lagos is one of Africa's biggest cities.

items.name: Norway
facts: Norway is known for deep coastal fjords.
It is one of the northern countries in Europe.

items.name: Peru
facts: Peru is home to Machu Picchu.
The Andes Mountains cross Peru.

items.name: Saudi Arabia
facts: Much of Saudi Arabia has desert land.
It is one of the larger countries in the Middle East.

items.name: South Korea
facts: South Korea is known for technology and busy cities.
Seoul is one of Asia's major cities.

items.name: Spain
facts: Spain is on the Iberian Peninsula in Europe.
It is known for art, football, and sunny coasts.

items.name: Switzerland
facts: Switzerland is known for the Alps mountains.
It is famous for watches and chocolate.

items.name: Thailand
facts: Thailand is known for tropical beaches and temples.
Bangkok is a very large city in Southeast Asia.

items.name: Turkey
facts: Turkey sits between Europe and Asia.
Istanbul is one of its best-known cities.

items.name: Chile
facts: Chile is a long, narrow country on South America's west coast.
The Andes Mountains run along much of Chile.

items.name: United Arab Emirates
facts: The United Arab Emirates includes cities like Dubai and Abu Dhabi.
Much of the country has desert climate.

items.name: South Africa
facts: South Africa is at the southern tip of Africa.
It is known for wildlife and varied landscapes.

items.name: United Kingdom
facts: The United Kingdom includes England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland.
London is famous for Big Ben and red buses.

items.name: United States
facts: The United States has 50 states.
It stretches from the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific Ocean.
